Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: There are several cases C001 to C005 each with activities like ApplicationReceived DataValidation PreliminaryScoring ManualReview and FinalDecision Each row has attributes like CaseID Activity Timestamp LocalResident CommunityGroup PreliminaryScore ScoreAdjustment ManualReview Decision Resource

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main question is where bias manifests They mention considering implications for people without community affiliations or geographic characteristics So I need to look at how attributes like LocalResident and CommunityGroup affect the scores and decisions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C001 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 710 ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Community Adjusted Score 720 Decision Approved Case C002 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 720 ScoreAdjustment 0 Adjusted Score 720 Decision Approved Case C003 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 715 ScoreAdjustment 0 Adjusted Score 715 Decision Rejected Case C004 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 690 ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Community Adjusted Score 700 Decision Approved Case C005 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 740 ScoreAdjustment 0 Adjusted Score 740 Decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The ScoreAdjustment is plus 10 for cases with CommunityGroup C001 and C004 both part of Highland Civic Darts Club For others it is 0 Also LocalResident is TRUE for C001 C002 C004 and FALSE for C003 C005

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: So the threshold for approval seems to be around 700 Because C004 adjusted score is 700 and it is approved C003 has 715 and is rejected

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets list all the adjusted scores and decisions C001 720 Approved C002 720 Approved C003 715 Rejected C004 700 Approved C005 740 Approved

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: That seems inconsistent Unless there are other factors Maybe the ManualReview step has some discretion In C003 ManualReview was done by Reviewer 4 and the decision was Rejected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main point is that CommunityGroup membership gives a plus 10 score adjustment So if two applicants have the same preliminary score the one in the community group gets a higher adjusted score

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: CommunityGroup is only present for Highland Civic Darts Club So maybe that group gets a plus 10 So the adjustment is based on community affiliation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Maybe the community group is tied to being a local resident Because Highland Civic Darts Club sounds like a local community So perhaps only local residents can be part of that group

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If that is the case then the plus 10 adjustment is only available to locals who are part of that group So non locals cannot get the adjustment which puts them at a disadvantage

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Alternatively maybe the threshold is different based on LocalResident status with non locals requiring a higher score

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That fits So if the threshold is different based on LocalResident status with non locals requiring a higher score that is a form of bias

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: All locals C001 C002 C004 are approved Non locals C003 rejected C005 approved C005 has a high score 740 so it is approved C003 has 715 which might be below the non local threshold

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias manifests in two ways Direct score adjustment for community group membership which seems to be available only to locals and possibly a higher approval threshold for non locals as inferred from C003 rejection despite a score higher than some approved locals

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The clearest evidence is the ScoreAdjustment for CommunityGroup That is a direct plus 10 for being in a specific group which likely favors a certain demographic locals in this case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Non locals do not have access to such community adjustments so they cannot get that boost Therefore a non local with a preliminary score of 690 would have 690 while a local in the group would have 700 leading to approval for the local and possible rejection for the non local

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Individuals without community affiliations like non locals or those not in specific groups need higher preliminary scores to achieve the same adjusted score as affiliated individuals This reduces fairness as two applicants with similar creditworthiness may have different outcomes based on group membership
